title: Week 10
summary:
Capesize
A good volume of fixing in the East and on one day alone over a dozen ships fixed from west Australia to China, but rates remained locked around $4.50. These levels showed very poor returns on time charter and although owners were prepared to fix voyage business they would not do the equivalent time charter rate. An Eco 180,000 toner fixed a round in the East at $5,000 daily.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: A good volume of fixing in the East and on one day alone over a dozen ships fixed from west Australia to China, but rates remained locked around $4.50. These levels showed very poor returns on time charter and although owners were prepared to fix voyage business they would not do the equivalent time charter rate.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: Atlantic trading remained very slow with new business this week failing to absorb much of the early tonnage. There was some pressure for charterers with US Gulf cargoes but here too the market now appeared evenly matched.

children.title: Supramax / Handysize
children.summary: A more encouraging week for the owners as rates started to improve in most areas. In the US-Gulf a nicely described 2012 built 58,600 dwt unit was booked for a trip to Singapore-Japan at about $13,000 daily or maybe a shade less.
